text.title: Five Finding Classes That Always Flag
text.summary: Five finding classes — SAST without fix block, secrets, unpatched CVEs, license conflicts, complexity hotspots — always flag for human review and never enter the agent's autofix branch.

section.title: Always-flag list
section.p: The four-condition predicate's "fail to flag" branch resolves to one of five canonical classes. Encoding the list as a closed enumeration prevents the agent from inventing new "I'll just patch this" categories.

rule.statement: The agent MUST flag (PR review comment or draft PR, never an in-loop commit) any finding belonging to: (1) SAST findings (CodeQL, Semgrep without `fix:`, Snyk Code) — propose a patch as a suggestion, human reviews; (2) secrets (gitleaks, trufflehog) — ROTATE first via 1Password CLI or vault before any cleanup commit; (3) CVEs (Snyk, Dependabot, Trivy) with no patched version — human picks: replace dep, accept risk with sunset date, or defer; (4) license conflicts (FOSSA, licensee) — ALWAYS human escalation, never bypass; (5) SonarQube cognitive-complexity hotspots — propose refactor as draft PR, human approves.
rule.rationale: These five classes share a property: the cost of an incorrect autofix is non-local (security exposure, legal liability, dependency-chain breakage) and cannot be reverted by `git revert` cleanly. The five-class list mirrors faion-network's existing memory rules `feedback_no_publish_without_image` (always-human gate) and `feedback_granular_commits` (one logical change per commit), and it is the same boundary CodeMender, Snyk Agent Fix, and Sonar's MCP loop already enforce.

example.code: # secret found by gitleaks pre-commit hook [gitleaks] AWS access key in src/cfg.py:14 agent action: rotate via 1Password CLI; await new key; remove from working tree; commit deletion with note "rotated <date>" and link to incident.
example.note: Rotate before cleanup — the leaked key is the threat, not the diff.

example.code: # same secret, but the agent silently strips it from the file agent action: ruff check --fix && git commit -m "chore: remove unused var"
example.note: The key is still live at AWS; the commit message lies about the change.

antipattern.description: Letting the agent auto-bump a dependency to "fix" a CVE when no patched version exists.
antipattern.reason: The agent picks a pre-release or a fork without security review; the CVE is "closed" but the supply-chain risk is now larger.

section.reference: Decision table mapping tool+finding to autofix/flag/escalate.
Snyk — Agent Fix and the autofix-vs-flag boundary.
Sonar AI Code Assurance — gate that flags rather than auto-fixes.
